summary: As the conflict enters day 30, expert consensus has shifted to recognize that regime change is unlikely to occur despite devastating military damage to Iranian capabilities. The Houthis have now joined the conflict, Gulf states view Iran as a direct threat while opposing continued war, and diplomatic off-ramps remain uncertain despite tentative negotiations.

Iran-backed Houthis entered the conflict on March 28 with ballistic missile attacks on Israel, escalating the regional scope; expert consensus remains heavily opposed (95% of academics, 56% of US public) with deep skepticism about regime change prospects; new diplomatic initiatives emerging from Pakistan alongside continued economic warnings from international bodies.

As of March 28 (Day 29), the conflict has intensified with tit-for-tat energy infrastructure strikes, Trump halting attacks on Iranian power plants for 10 days, Pentagon considering 10,000 additional troop deployments, and academic expert consensus showing 72% opposition to the war—far exceeding initial projections. Iran dismissed a ceasefire proposal on March 25 and continues regional escalation despite massive military losses.

By Day 28, military momentum favors US-Israel operations with 70% of Iranian missile launchers destroyed and 90% decline in Iranian strike capability, but civilian casualties exceed 1,900 in Iran and 1,100+ in Lebanon, driving domestic US opposition to 59-64%, while diplomatic talks remain stalled despite Trump's April 6 deadline extension.

As of day 27, the conflict continues with sustained strikes by US-Israel forces that have degraded Iranian military capacity by an estimated 80-90% across multiple domains, while international reactions remain divided between acknowledgment of military effectiveness and serious concerns about 1,500+ civilian deaths, regional spillover into Lebanon (1,000+ casualties), global economic disruption, and the lack of a clear diplomatic resolution path. The death of Supreme Leader Khamenei and appointment of his son as successor have created new uncertainty about Iran's future trajectory.

Day 26: Trump paused planned power grid strikes claiming progress in talks, but Iran denies negotiations. Lebanon expelled Iranian ambassador; Kuwait airport hit by drone fire. Lebanese death toll exceeds 1,070. Iran casualty count at 1,500+ dead. Global energy crisis deepening with IEA calling it worse than 1970s crises. Expert consensus remains largely opposed due to unclear endgame and civilian toll, though military degradation of Iranian capabilities acknowledged.

Major developments on March 23-24: Trump announced a 5-day pause on power grid strikes citing claimed negotiations with Iran, which Tehran denies; IEA warned the economic impact is worse than the 1970s oil crises combined; "unprecedented" Israeli strikes continue on Tehran; confirmed civilian death toll in Iran has surpassed 1,000+. International consensus remains divided but leans opposed, with legal experts broadly condemning the operation as violating international law.

Day 24: Trump's 48-hour ultimatum to strike Iranian power plants expires tonight if Hormuz isn't reopened. Iran responded by threatening to permanently close the strait and mine the Gulf. Israel launched new "wide-scale" strikes on Tehran. IEA chief warned this is worse than 1970s oil crises combined. UK PM Starmer called emergency meeting as economic fallout mounts. Death toll in Iran exceeds 1,500.

Major escalation on March 21-22: Iranian missiles struck Dimona and Arad near Israel's nuclear facility, injuring 180+ people after defense failures. Trump issued a 48-hour ultimatum threatening to "obliterate" Iran's power plants if the Strait of Hormuz isn't reopened; Iran countered by threatening all U.S. energy infrastructure in the region. Conflicting signals as Trump discusses "winding down" while Israel announces strikes will "increase significantly."

Day 21-22 updates: Netanyahu halts energy strikes after Trump pressure over South Pars attack, exposing US-Israel tension; Trump lifts Iran oil sanctions temporarily and considers "winding down" while sending more Marines; Houthis announce they're considering Bab al-Mandab blockade; IAEA warns nuclear material will survive conflict; experts increasingly describe war as open-ended with no clear exit strategy.

Major escalation on Day 20-21: Israel struck Iran's South Pars gas field, prompting Iranian retaliation against Gulf energy facilities including Qatar's Ras Laffan. Netanyahu agreed to halt energy attacks after Trump intervention, though both denied US prior knowledge despite contradicting sources. Oil hit $115/barrel; no ceasefire in sight as Iran rejects negotiations and warns of 'zero restraint.'

Day 20: Israel killed security chief Ali Larijani and intelligence minister Khatib; Iran retaliated by striking Qatar's Ras Laffan gas terminal and Saudi refineries after Israel hit Iran's South Pars field. Oil prices surged to $116/barrel. Qatar expelled Iranian attachés. Former Trump counterterrorism chief Joe Kent resigned citing no evidence of imminent threat. Death toll in Lebanon nearing 1,000 as conflict widens.

Day 19 update: Israel claims killing of Iran's Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib; 2 killed in Iranian missile strike on Ramat Gan, Israel; continued Iranian drone/missile attacks on Gulf states with intercepts reported across Saudi Arabia, UAE, Qatar, and Kuwait; U.S. dropped 5,000-lb bombs on Iranian missile sites near Strait of Hormuz; Iranian casualties now exceed 1,444 killed.

Day 17 updates: Death toll exceeds 2,300 across region including 200+ children in Iran. Washington Post reports U.S. intelligence assesses Iran's regime will survive "weakened but more hard-line." EU declined Trump's request to help secure Strait of Hormuz. Senior Basij commander and militia figures killed in overnight U.S.-Israeli strikes. Iran's FM rejects ceasefire, demands guarantees and reparations before any talks.

Day 16: Iran's FM Araghchi denied ceasefire requests on CBS, stating Iran is "ready to defend ourselves as long as it takes." Israel reports it needs 3+ more weeks; US expects conflict to end in "weeks." Oil prices hit $105/barrel (24% US gas price increase). Polling shows 54% US disapproval. Expert consensus remains divided—military objectives advancing but unclear endgame and regime resilience concerns growing.

Day 16 updates: US struck 90+ military targets on Kharg Island (Iran's oil export hub) while warning oil infrastructure could be next; Iran FM Araghchi stated war can only end with guarantees and compensation; heavy airstrikes reported across multiple Iranian cities early March 15; TRIP expert poll shows 86% of IR scholars oppose war while only 10% support; conflict costs reach $16.5B with US claiming 90% reduction in Iran's missile capability.

Day 14 updates: US struck Kharg Island (Iran's main oil hub), with Hegseth reporting 95% reduction in Iranian drone capability. Iran's new supreme leader reportedly wounded. 1,444 killed in Iran per health ministry. US deploying 2,200 additional Marines. First French military casualty. 250+ US organizations calling to halt war funding. Oil prices remain over $100/barrel with Strait of Hormuz closed.

Day 14 (March 13): Israel launched "extensive wave" of 200+ strikes on Tehran during Al Quds Day; first French soldier killed in Iraqi Kurdistan; US KC-135 crash killed 4 (total US deaths now 11); 3.2 million displaced in Iran per UN; Goldman Sachs forecasts 20% higher oil prices; international legal consensus remains that attacks violate international law; expert analysis increasingly questions unclear endgame and warns of potential ground troop requirement.

Day 12 updates: Iran demands ceasefire guarantee of no future attacks; Russia reportedly providing tactical drone advice to Iran; IEA releasing record 400M barrels of oil; approximately 140 U.S. troops wounded; ongoing Strait of Hormuz attacks on commercial shipping; Spain withdrew ambassador to Israel; WHO warns of toxic 'black rain' from fuel depot strikes.

Day 12 updates: US launched "most intense day" of strikes; Iran threatens to target regional banks and financial centers; 140 US troops wounded, 8 killed; ~1,300 Iranian civilians dead; 700,000 displaced in Lebanon; Iran's internet blackout now 240+ hours; Senate Democrats demand public hearings on war; investigation ongoing into school strike killing ~175 children.

Day 11 updates: Defense Secretary Hegseth announced Tuesday will bring "most intense strikes" yet; Iran's IRGC claims continued missile capability with 1+ tonne warheads; Trump characterized war as "short-term excursion" but gave no timeline; 8th US soldier death confirmed; residential building strike in Arak killed 5; oil prices stabilized around $90 after briefly hitting $120; Iran FM Araghchi rejected Trump's quick-end claims, says Iran will fight "as long as necessary."

Major update: Mojtaba Khamenei was named Iran's new Supreme Leader on March 8, 2026, signaling hardline continuity and defiance of US demands. Death toll in Iran has risen to over 1,332, US casualties to 8 service members. Expert/public opposition to the war remains high (~72-85% among scholars, 75% among US public), while Israeli public support is strong at 82%. Oil prices have spiked above $114/barrel with warnings of potential global recession.

Day 9 updates: Israel struck Tehran oil depots for the first time; Iranian drone damaged Bahrain desalination plant; IDF targeted IRGC Quds Force commanders in Beirut hotel strike (4 killed); Iran's President Pezeshkian apologized to Gulf neighbors but attacks continued; Trump escalated rhetoric warning Iran will be "hit very hard"; CENTCOM reports 3,000+ targets struck and 43 ships destroyed; Foreign Policy survey shows overwhelming expert disapproval of the war; death toll in Iran now at least 1,332.

Day 8 updates: War enters second week with Trump demanding "unconditional surrender" and Iran refusing negotiations. Total death toll reaches 1,332+ in Iran (181 children). UK now allowing US strikes from British bases. New Foreign Policy survey shows IR scholars overwhelmingly oppose the war, believing it harms US security. Russia reportedly providing Iran intelligence on US positions. Oil prices up 24-31% and economic disruption mounting.

Day 7 updates: US reports 90% reduction in Iranian missile capability after B-2 strikes; over 20 Iranian ships sunk including historic submarine torpedo attack on IRIS Dena. France authorized base use; Italy's defense minister called operation illegal despite sending defensive aid. Death toll in Iran exceeds 1,200. Expert consensus remains divided with concerns about unclear endgame and constitutional issues despite military effectiveness.

Day 6 updates: Death toll in Iran rises to 1,230+; US Senate votes 53-47 to reject War Powers resolution blocking the war; IRIS Dena frigate sunk by US submarine (first US sub kill since WWII); Iran rejects negotiations; international legal consensus strongly condemns strikes as UN Charter violations; only 25% of Americans support the war per new polling; conflict spreads to Lebanon with 50+ killed in Israeli strikes.

Day 5 updates: Iran death toll surpassed 1,145; Turkish NATO systems intercepted Iranian missile in Mediterranean; IRIS Dena frigate sunk near Sri Lanka (100+ crew unaccounted for); funeral ceremonies for Khamenei underway; IRGC announced ground forces entering operations; CNN poll shows 60% American disapproval; experts increasingly skeptical regime change achievable via airpower alone.

Day 4 updates: Death toll in Iran rises to 787; US casualties to 6 killed. Israel launches ground invasion of Lebanon opening new front. Iran declares Strait of Hormuz "closed," threatening ships; shipping effectively halted, oil up 13%, gas up 40-50%. NATO chief expresses support; China/Russia condemn attacks as illegal. Strike on IRIB headquarters, parliament building; Assembly of Experts bombed during supreme leader election meeting. Expert consensus remains sharply divided on whether airpower can achieve regime change.
